content.text:
Based on my examination of the molecule from multiple angles, I can clearly identify the neighbor atoms of atom index 9 (C:9).

**Atom 9 (Carbon) is covalently connected to 4 neighbor atoms:**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 7 | C (Carbon) |
| 8 | N (Nitrogen) |
| 10 | O (Oxygen) |
| 12 | C (Carbon) |

**Summary:**
- The central carbon atom C:9 is bonded to:
  - **C:7** - A carbon atom on the left side (part of the six-membered ring)
  - **N:8** - A nitrogen atom (bridgehead atom connecting the two rings)
  - **O:10** - An oxygen atom (carbonyl oxygen, double bonded)
  - **C:12** - A carbon atom on the right side (part of the side chain)

**geomconf_index:** 5446

This structure appears to be an amide linkage where the carbonyl carbon (C:9) is bonded to a nitrogen (N:8), a carbonyl oxygen (O:10), and two carbon atoms (C:7 and C:12) forming part of a larger molecule with fused ring systems.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
